title: Podman 4.6.1 fails when starting container with --cpu option
body:
### Issue Description

I have a RHEL 8 machine. Recently I updated podman to the latest version(4.4.1 -> 4.6.1). After that, every time I start container with `--cpu=N` option, it fails with an error.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run `podman run --cpus=2 -p 1521:1521 -e ORACLE_PASSWORD=mypass docker.io/gvenzl/oracle-free:23-slim-faststart 
` on RHEL 8 machine


### Describe the results you received

```
Resource limits are not supported and ignored on cgroups V1 rootless systems
Error: OCI runtime error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: error during container init: error setting cgroup config for procHooks process: cannot set cpu limit: container could not join or create cgroup
```

### Describe the results you expected

Same as on 4.4.1 — container starts succesfully.

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

The host is running on OpenStack

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
This is intentional. See #15384. 

>I updated podman to the latest version(4.4.1 -> 4.6.1).

Are you really sure? The warning has been present since v4.3.0. And it's not a podman limitation, it's a cgroups v1 limitation. Absolutely nothing podman can do about it.

Is it possible that you were running as root before?

@edsantiago linked PR mentions "warning", not "error". When running on 4.4.1 there is indeed the warning[1], but the container doesn't fail to start[2]

UPD: regarding root: there is `podman info` for both old and new machines, so you can check yourself. The podman was started in the same way on both machines.

[1] `Resource limits are not supported and ignored on cgroups V1 rootless systems`
[2] There is no `Error: OCI runtime error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: error during container init: error setting cgroup config for procHooks process: cannot set cpu limit: container could not join or create cgroup` part

I am not well versed in go, but it looks like the problem was caused by this commit[1], which removed `s.ResourceLimits = nil` part so even if limits do not make sense for cgroups v1, they are being enforced anyway.
This was later fixed[2], but it seems, that the fix didn't make it into 4.6.2[3]. Is it possible to backport it to 4.6 branch?

FYI @edsantiago 

[1] https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/4f5f89cf88714d791f3da72ecaa660a1b7038375
[2]  https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19707
[3] https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.6.2/pkg/specgen/generate/validate.go#L32

Thank you for your followup. I did not take a close enough look at your report, and I apologize.

For RHEL support, please go through your support channels. (That used to include [bugzilla](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/enter_bug.cgi?classification=Red%20Hat), but I don't know how to do it these days). I expect a RHEL8 backport to be unlikely, but will reopen this issue for the time being to allow discussion.

Yes for RHEL you have to go through the Red Hat support channels to report bugs/request backports, https://access.redhat.com/ would likely be a good start. Note backports generally need a strong justification, i.e. why can't you just remove --cpus=2 from the cli as this should work?

I am going to close this as there is nothing to do for us upstream as it is fixed in the latest version but feel free to continue the conversation.
